avtpComponents.name: instrText
avtpComponents.text: In this experiment, a green circle will appear on the screen. Your goal is to press the SPACEBAR as quickly as possible when you see it. Keep your finger on the spacebar and focus on the center cross.

avtpComponents.name: fbEarlyText
avtpComponents.text: Too Early! Wait for the circle.

avtpComponents.name: fbSlowText
avtpComponents.text: Too Slow! Try to be faster.

avtpComponents.name: mainInstrText
avtpComponents.text: The main experiment begins now. There will be 50 trials. No feedback will be given. Remember: Focus and react as fast as possible.

avtpComponents.name: endText
avtpComponents.text: Experiment Complete. Thank you for your participation.

tasks.name: Stroop Task for Color-Word Interference
tasks.messages:
Describe your experiment requirements. The AI will first generate a detailed description, then create the flowchart JSON.

test

Starting experiment design with qwen3.5-plus...
Thinking completed

  1. Analyze the Request:

    • Role: Psychology Experiment Design Expert.
    • Task: Analyze user requirements and generate a concise experiment description.
    • User Input: "test"
    • Constraints:
      • Search for relevant methodologies (internal knowledge).
      • Generate a concise description including specific sections (Title/Purpose, Design/Procedure, Variables, Trial Structure, Stimuli, Response, Logic, Timing).
      • Structure must include: (1) Instruction for entire experiment, (2) Practice Loop, (3) Instruction for Main Experiment, (4) Main Experiment Loop, (5) Ending.
      • CRITICAL: The Experiment Summary must be a ONE-SENTENCE summary at the very beginning, under 50 characters, formatted as **Experiment Summary:** [Summary].
    • Problem: The user input is just "test". This is not a specific experiment requirement. It's likely a placeholder or a request to demonstrate the format with a generic/common experiment.
    • Strategy: Since there's no specific topic, I should select a classic, well-known psychological paradigm to demonstrate the format correctly. The Stroop Task is a standard choice for cognitive psychology experiments. I will design a standard Color-Word Stroop Task.

  2. Drafting the Experiment Summary:

    • Needs to be under 50 characters.
    • Needs to be one sentence.
    • Format: **Experiment Summary:** [Summary]
    • Draft 1: Experiment Summary: Classic Stroop Task Measuring Interference Effects (59 chars) - Too long.
    • Draft 2: Experiment Summary: Stroop Task Measuring Color-Word Interference (54 chars) - Too long.
    • Draft 3: Experiment Summary: Stroop Task Assessing Color-Word Interference (51 chars) - Too long.
    • Draft 4: Experiment Summary: Stroop Task for Color-Word Interference (44 chars) - Good.
    • Draft 5: Experiment Summary: Visual Stroop Task Measuring Cognitive Interference (52 chars) - Too long.
    • Selection: Experiment Summary: Stroop Task for Color-Word Interference

  3. **

Outlining completed

Experiment Summary: Stroop Task for Color-Word Interference

1. Instruction for Entire Experiment
Participants are welcomed and informed that the study investigates reaction time and attention. They are instructed to sit comfortably, minimize head movement, and respond as quickly and accurately as possible using the keyboard. They are told they will see color words printed in colored ink and must identify the ink color, ignoring the word meaning.

2. Practice Loop

  • Purpose: To familiarize participants with the task mechanics and key mappings without data recording.
  • Trials: 10 trials.
  • Feedback: Immediate feedback (Correct/Incorrect) is displayed after each response.
  • Condition: Balanced congruent (word matches ink) and incongruent (word differs from ink) trials.
  • Termination: Loop ends after 10 trials; participants proceed automatically.

3. Instruction for Main Experiment
Participants are reminded to prioritize speed and accuracy. They are informed that feedback will no longer be provided during this section. They are instructed to press the spacebar to begin when ready.

4. Main Experiment Loop

  • Design: Within-subjects design manipulating congruency.
  • Trials: 100 trials total (50 Congruent, 50 Incongruent), randomized sequence.
  • Trial Structure:
    1. Fixation: Central cross (+) displayed for 500ms.
    2. Stimulus: Color word (e.g., "RED", "BLUE") displayed in colored ink (Red, Blue, Green, Yellow) until response or max 2000ms.
    3. Inter-Trial Interval (ITI): Blank screen for 500ms.
  • Stimulus Details: Words "RED", "BLUE", "GREEN", "YELLOW" presented in Arial font, size 48, centered on a white background.
  • Response Collection: Four key press mapping (e.g., d=Red, f=Blue, j=Green, k=Yellow). Reaction time (ms) and accuracy (0/1) recorded.
  • Branching Logic: None; all participants complete all trials.

5. Ending
Upon completion of the main loop, a "Thank You" screen is displayed. Participants are debriefed about the purpose of the Stroop effect (interference between automatic reading and color naming). They are asked if they have questions before being dismissed.

Outline completed. Now generating the flowchart based on the experiment description...
Thinking completed

  1. Analyze the Request:

    • Role: Psychology experiment designer.
    • Task: Generate a complete JSON data structure for a PsyClaw flowchart project based on the provided experiment description (Stroop Task).
    • Constraints:
      • ONLY valid JSON following the provided schema.
      • VARIABLE FORMAT: Use $ at the START (e.g., $word, $color).
      • Point system: Routine IDs are EVEN (2, 4, 6...).
      • CONDITION NAMES: Descriptive names (e.g., "Congruent Red", "Incongruent Blue").
      • ROUTINE COMPONENTS: Each routine MUST have at least one component.
      • NO FEEDBACK: Do not include feedback routines or components indicating correct/incorrect.
      • NO thinking process, explanations, markdown - ONLY raw JSON.
    • Schema: PsyClaw Flowchart Schema v2.0.0.

  2. Analyze the Experiment Description:

    • Task: Stroop Task (Color-Word Interference).
    • Flow:
      1. Instruction (Welcome/Task Info).
      2. Practice Loop (10 trials, balanced congruent/incongruent). Note: Schema says NO FEEDBACK. The description mentions feedback for practice, but the IMPORTANT RULES say "NO FEEDBACK: Do not include feedback routines or components that indicate whether the participant's response was correct or incorrect." I must follow the IMPORTANT RULES over the experiment description regarding feedback.
      3. Instruction (Main Exp Info).
      4. Main Experiment Loop (100 trials, 50 Congruent, 50 Incongruent, randomized).
        • Trial Structure: Fixation (500ms) -> Stimulus (Word/Color) -> ITI (500ms).
        • Response: Keys d, f, j, k mapped to Red, Blue, Green, Yellow.
      5. Ending (Thank You/Debrief).
    • Variables:
      • $word: Text content (RED, BLUE, GREEN, YELLOW).
      • $color: Ink color (red, blue, green, yellow).
Flowchart completed

avtpComponents.name: instruction_text
avtpComponents.text:
Welcome to the Stroop Task.

Your task is to identify the COLOR of the text displayed on the screen.
Ignore the meaning of the word itself.

Press SPACE to begin practice.

avtpComponents.name: main_instr_text
avtpComponents.text:
Practice Complete.

Now we will begin the main experiment.
Remember: Press F for Red, J for Blue, K for Green, L for Yellow.
Do not worry about the word meaning, only the ink color.

Press SPACE to start.

avtpComponents.name: thank_you_text
avtpComponents.text:
Thank you for participating!

You have completed the Stroop Color-Word Interference Task.
